+// Dotfiles-managed Firefox preferences.
+// Installed into the default local Firefox profile by scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh.
+// This file intentionally does not manage cookies, history, sessions, or logins.
+
+// Required for Granted/AWS console isolation via Firefox containers.
+user_pref("privacy.userContext.enabled", true);
+user_pref("privacy.userContext.ui.enabled", true);
+user_pref("privacy.userContext.longPressBehavior", 2);
+
+// Keep startup/new-tab noise low without changing browsing data.
+user_pref("browser.shell.checkDefaultBrowser", false);
+user_pref("browser.startup.homepage_override.mstone", "ignore");
+user_pref("browser.bookmarks.restore_default_bookmarks", false);
+user_pref("browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.feeds.telemetry", false);
+user_pref("browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.showSponsored", false);
+user_pref("browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.showSponsoredTopSites", false);
+user_pref("browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.telemetry", false);
+
+// Mirror the lightweight privacy defaults from Firefox enterprise policies.
+user_pref("app.shield.optoutstudies.enabled", false);
+user_pref("datareporting.healthreport.uploadEnabled", false);
+user_pref("datareporting.policy.dataSubmissionEnabled", false);
+user_pref("extensions.pocket.enabled", false);
+user_pref("toolkit.telemetry.enabled", false);
+user_pref("toolkit.telemetry.unified", false);

Index: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh
===================================================================
--- /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh
+++ /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,227 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Apply dotfiles-managed Firefox policy and default-profile preferences on macOS.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+FIREFOX_APP="${FIREFOX_APP:-/Applications/Firefox.app}"
+FIREFOX_ROOT="${FIREFOX_ROOT:-$HOME/Library/Application Support/Firefox}"
+POLICY_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_POLICY_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/policies.json}"
+USER_JS_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_USER_JS_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/user.js}"
+
+BLUE='\033[0;34m'
+GREEN='\033[0;32m'
+YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
+NC='\033[0m'
+
+log_info() {
+    echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $1"
+}
+
+log_success() {
+    echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"
+}
+
+log_warning() {
+    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARNING]${NC} $1"
+}
+
+backup_existing_file() {
+    local target_path="$1"
+    local backup_path="${target_path}.backup.$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
+
+    cp "$target_path" "$backup_path"
+    log_info "Backed up existing $(basename "$target_path") to $backup_path"
+}
+
+copy_managed_file() {
+    local source_path="$1"
+    local target_path="$2"
+    local label="$3"
+
+    if [[ ! -f "$source_path" ]]; then
+        log_warning "$label source missing: $source_path"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -f "$target_path" ]] && cmp -s "$source_path" "$target_path"; then
+        log_success "$label already current"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -e "$target_path" && ! -f "$target_path" && ! -L "$target_path" ]]; then
+        log_warning "$label target exists but is not a file: $target_path"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if ! mkdir -p "$(dirname "$target_path")" 2>/dev/null; then
+        log_warning "Could not create $(dirname "$target_path")"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -f "$target_path" || -L "$target_path" ]]; then
+        backup_existing_file "$target_path"
+        rm -f "$target_path"
+    fi
+
+    if cp "$source_path" "$target_path"; then
+        chmod 0644 "$target_path" 2>/dev/null || true
+        log_success "Installed $label"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    log_warning "Could not install $label to $target_path"
+    return 1
+}
+
+resolve_profile_path() {
+    local profile_ref="$1"
+
+    if [[ "$profile_ref" == /* ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$profile_ref"
+    else
+        printf '%s/%s\n' "$FIREFOX_ROOT" "$profile_ref"
+    fi
+}
+
+find_default_profile() {
+    local profiles_ini="$FIREFOX_ROOT/profiles.ini"
+    local section=""
+    local path=""
+    local is_relative="1"
+    local is_default="0"
+    local install_default=""
+    local profile_default=""
+    local profile_default_relative="1"
+    local first_profile=""
+    local first_profile_relative="1"
+
+    [[ -f "$profiles_ini" ]] || return 1
+
+    commit_profile_section() {
+        if [[ "$section" != Profile* || -z "$path" ]]; then
+            return 0
+        fi
+
+        if [[ -z "$first_profile" ]]; then
+            first_profile="$path"
+            first_profile_relative="$is_relative"
+        fi
+
+        if [[ "$is_default" == "1" ]]; then
+            profile_default="$path"
+            profile_default_relative="$is_relative"
+        fi
+    }
+
+    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
+        line="${line%$'\r'}"
+
+        case "$line" in
+        '' | \#* | \;*)
+            continue
+            ;;
+        \[*\])
+            commit_profile_section
+            section="${line#[}"
+            section="${section%]}"
+            path=""
+            is_relative="1"
+            is_default="0"
+            continue
+            ;;
+        esac
+
+        case "$section" in
+        Install*)
+            case "$line" in
+            Default=*) install_default="${line#Default=}" ;;
+            esac
+            ;;
+        Profile*)
+            case "$line" in
+            Path=*) path="${line#Path=}" ;;
+            IsRelative=*) is_relative="${line#IsRelative=}" ;;
+            Default=1) is_default="1" ;;
+            esac
+            ;;
+        esac
+    done <"$profiles_ini"
+
+    commit_profile_section
+
+    if [[ -n "$install_default" ]]; then
+        resolve_profile_path "$install_default"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -n "$profile_default" ]]; then
+        if [[ "$profile_default_relative" == "1" ]]; then
+            resolve_profile_path "$profile_default"
+        else
+            printf '%s\n' "$profile_default"
+        fi
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -n "$first_profile" ]]; then
+        if [[ "$first_profile_relative" == "1" ]]; then
+            resolve_profile_path "$first_profile"
+        else
+            printf '%s\n' "$first_profile"
+        fi
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+install_policy() {
+    local policy_target="$FIREFOX_APP/Contents/Resources/distribution/policies.json"
+
+    if [[ ! -d "$FIREFOX_APP" ]]; then
+        log_warning "Firefox.app not found at $FIREFOX_APP; skipping enterprise policy"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if copy_managed_file "$POLICY_SOURCE" "$policy_target" "Firefox policies.json"; then
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    log_warning "Manual policy install: sudo mkdir -p \"$(dirname "$policy_target")\" && sudo cp \"$POLICY_SOURCE\" \"$policy_target\""
+    return 0
+}
+
+install_user_js() {
+    local profile_path=""
+
+    if ! profile_path="$(find_default_profile)"; then
+        log_warning "No Firefox profile found; run Firefox once, then rerun setup to install user.js"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ ! -d "$profile_path" ]]; then
+        log_warning "Default Firefox profile directory does not exist: $profile_path"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    copy_managed_file "$USER_JS_SOURCE" "$profile_path/user.js" "Firefox default-profile user.js" || true
+}
+
+main() {
+    if [[ "$(uname -s)" != "Darwin" ]]; then
+        log_info "Firefox setup is macOS-only; skipping"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        python3 -m json.tool "$POLICY_SOURCE" >/dev/null
+    fi
+
+    install_policy
+    install_user_js
+
+    log_success "Firefox dotfiles setup complete"
+}
+
+main "$@"

+## Configuration Files
+
+### `.granted/config`
+
+Main Granted configuration:
+

+- Points Granted at `/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/MacOS/firefox`.

+
+Maps AWS profile names to Firefox container colors and icons:
+
+```text
 production=red:briefcase
+prod=red:briefcase
 development=green:tree
-staging=yellow:cart
+dev=green:tree

+

 ## Troubleshooting
 
 ### Autocomplete Shows Wrong Items

-### No Colors/Icons in Firefox
-**Problem:** Firefox tabs are plain, no colors or icons
-**Solution:**

+### No Colors Or Icons In Firefox
 
+Problem: Firefox tabs are plain, with no Granted colors or icons.
+
+Solution:
+
+1. Check `about:policies` for the Granted extension policy.
+2. Confirm the Granted Firefox addon is installed: `about:addons`.
+3. Ensure Firefox is set as Granted's browser: `granted browser set firefox`.
+4. Check profile mappings in `~/.granted/firefox-profiles`.
+
+### About:Config Settings Did Not Apply
+
+Problem: Firefox preferences from dotfiles are missing.
+
+Solution:
+
+1. Quit Firefox completely.
+2. Run `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh`.
+3. Reopen Firefox.
+4. Check that the default profile has `user.js` under `~/Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles/<profile>/user.js`.
+
 ### Wrong Browser Opens
-**Problem:** Chrome or Safari opens instead of Firefox
-**Solution:**
+
+Problem: Chrome or Safari opens instead of Firefox.
+
+Solution:
+
 ```bash
 granted browser set firefox
 ```
 
 ### Profile Not Found
-**Problem:** "Profile not found" errors
-**Solution:**
-1. Check AWS configuration: `aws configure list-profiles`
-2. Verify AWS config file exists: `ls ~/.aws/config`
-3. Ensure profile names match exactly
 
+Problem: Granted reports profile-not-found errors.
+
+Solution:
+
+1. Check AWS configuration: `aws configure list-profiles`.
+2. Verify AWS config exists: `ls ~/.aws/config`.
+3. Ensure profile names match `.granted/firefox-profiles` exactly.
+
 ## Helper Scripts

+Use `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh` for Firefox policy and `user.js` setup.

Index: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh
===================================================================
--- /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh
+++ /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,227 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Apply dotfiles-managed Firefox policy and default-profile preferences on macOS.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+FIREFOX_APP="${FIREFOX_APP:-/Applications/Firefox.app}"
+FIREFOX_ROOT="${FIREFOX_ROOT:-$HOME/Library/Application Support/Firefox}"
+POLICY_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_POLICY_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/policies.json}"
+USER_JS_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_USER_JS_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/user.js}"
+
+BLUE='\033[0;34m'
+GREEN='\033[0;32m'
+YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
+NC='\033[0m'
+
+log_info() {
+    echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $1"
+}
+
+log_success() {
+    echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"
+}
+
+log_warning() {
+    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARNING]${NC} $1"
+}
+
+backup_existing_file() {
+    local target_path="$1"
+    local backup_path="${target_path}.backup.$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
+
+    cp "$target_path" "$backup_path"
+    log_info "Backed up existing $(basename "$target_path") to $backup_path"
+}
+
+copy_managed_file() {
+    local source_path="$1"
+    local target_path="$2"
+    local label="$3"
+
+    if [[ ! -f "$source_path" ]]; then
+        log_warning "$label source missing: $source_path"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -f "$target_path" ]] && cmp -s "$source_path" "$target_path"; then
+        log_success "$label already current"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -e "$target_path" && ! -f "$target_path" && ! -L "$target_path" ]]; then
+        log_warning "$label target exists but is not a file: $target_path"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if ! mkdir -p "$(dirname "$target_path")" 2>/dev/null; then
+        log_warning "Could not create $(dirname "$target_path")"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -f "$target_path" || -L "$target_path" ]]; then
+        backup_existing_file "$target_path"
+        rm -f "$target_path"
+    fi
+
+    if cp "$source_path" "$target_path"; then
+        chmod 0644 "$target_path" 2>/dev/null || true
+        log_success "Installed $label"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    log_warning "Could not install $label to $target_path"
+    return 1
+}
+
+resolve_profile_path() {
+    local profile_ref="$1"
+
+    if [[ "$profile_ref" == /* ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$profile_ref"
+    else
+        printf '%s/%s\n' "$FIREFOX_ROOT" "$profile_ref"
+    fi
+}
+
+find_default_profile() {
+    local profiles_ini="$FIREFOX_ROOT/profiles.ini"
+    local section=""
+    local path=""
+    local is_relative="1"
+    local is_default="0"
+    local install_default=""
+    local profile_default=""
+    local profile_default_relative="1"
+    local first_profile=""
+    local first_profile_relative="1"
+
+    [[ -f "$profiles_ini" ]] || return 1
+
+    commit_profile_section() {
+        if [[ "$section" != Profile* || -z "$path" ]]; then
+            return 0
+        fi
+
+        if [[ -z "$first_profile" ]]; then
+            first_profile="$path"
+            first_profile_relative="$is_relative"
+        fi
+
+        if [[ "$is_default" == "1" ]]; then
+            profile_default="$path"
+            profile_default_relative="$is_relative"
+        fi
+    }
+
+    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
+        line="${line%$'\r'}"
+
+        case "$line" in
+        '' | \#* | \;*)
+            continue
+            ;;
+        \[*\])
+            commit_profile_section
+            section="${line#[}"
+            section="${section%]}"
+            path=""
+            is_relative="1"
+            is_default="0"
+            continue
+            ;;
+        esac
+
+        case "$section" in
+        Install*)
+            case "$line" in
+            Default=*) install_default="${line#Default=}" ;;
+            esac
+            ;;
+        Profile*)
+            case "$line" in
+            Path=*) path="${line#Path=}" ;;
+            IsRelative=*) is_relative="${line#IsRelative=}" ;;
+            Default=1) is_default="1" ;;
+            esac
+            ;;
+        esac
+    done <"$profiles_ini"
+
+    commit_profile_section
+
+    if [[ -n "$install_default" ]]; then
+        resolve_profile_path "$install_default"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -n "$profile_default" ]]; then
+        if [[ "$profile_default_relative" == "1" ]]; then
+            resolve_profile_path "$profile_default"
+        else
+            printf '%s\n' "$profile_default"
+        fi
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -n "$first_profile" ]]; then
+        if [[ "$first_profile_relative" == "1" ]]; then
+            resolve_profile_path "$first_profile"
+        else
+            printf '%s\n' "$first_profile"
+        fi
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+install_policy() {
+    local policy_target="$FIREFOX_APP/Contents/Resources/distribution/policies.json"
+
+    if [[ ! -d "$FIREFOX_APP" ]]; then
+        log_warning "Firefox.app not found at $FIREFOX_APP; skipping enterprise policy"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if copy_managed_file "$POLICY_SOURCE" "$policy_target" "Firefox policies.json"; then
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    log_warning "Manual policy install: sudo mkdir -p \"$(dirname "$policy_target")\" && sudo cp \"$POLICY_SOURCE\" \"$policy_target\""
+    return 0
+}
+
+install_user_js() {
+    local profile_path=""
+
+    if ! profile_path="$(find_default_profile)"; then
+        log_warning "No Firefox profile found; run Firefox once, then rerun setup to install user.js"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ ! -d "$profile_path" ]]; then
+        log_warning "Default Firefox profile directory does not exist: $profile_path"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    copy_managed_file "$USER_JS_SOURCE" "$profile_path/user.js" "Firefox default-profile user.js" || true
+}
+
+main() {
+    if [[ "$(uname -s)" != "Darwin" ]]; then
+        log_info "Firefox setup is macOS-only; skipping"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        python3 -m json.tool "$POLICY_SOURCE" >/dev/null
+    fi
+
+    install_policy
+    install_user_js
+
+    log_success "Firefox dotfiles setup complete"
+}
+
+main "$@"

+
 When you type `assume` followed by space and Tab:

-  - `development` → **Green tree** 🟢🌳

-## Setup Instructions
+### Firefox Browser Integration

+

+

+

+

 ```bash

 ```

+Setup does the following:

-   cd ~/dotfiles

+- Installs Firefox from `homebrew/Brewfile` and `phase_9_fonts_and_apps`.
+- Uses stow to sync `.granted/config` and `.granted/firefox-profiles` into `~/.granted`.

-   ```
+### Manual Fallback
 
-## Configuration Files
+If automatic Firefox policy installation is skipped because the app bundle is not writable:

-Main Granted configuration with:
-- **Firefox** as default browser
-- **Frequently-used** profile ordering  
-- **Caching enabled** for performance
-- **Telemetry disabled** for privacy

+```bash

 ```
+
+If the Granted extension is still missing, install it from AMO:
+
+```text
+https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/granted/
+```
+
+Check active Firefox enterprise policies at `about:policies`.
+
+## Configuration Files
+
+### `.granted/config`
+
+Main Granted configuration:
+
+- Sets Firefox as the default Granted browser.
+- Points Granted at `/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/MacOS/firefox`.
+- Uses the macOS keychain backend.
+
+### `.granted/firefox-profiles`
+
+Maps AWS profile names to Firefox container colors and icons:
+
+```text

 development=green:tree
-staging=yellow:cart
+dev=green:tree
+management=blue:briefcase
 security=purple:fingerprint

+Machine-level Firefox enterprise policies:

+### `scripts/setup/firefox/user.js`
+
+Default-profile `about:config` preferences:

+

 ## Troubleshooting

-**Problem:** `assume <TAB>` shows files or Granted commands instead of AWS profiles

-# Reload Fish completions

-### No Colors/Icons in Firefox
-**Problem:** Firefox tabs are plain, no colors or icons
-**Solution:**
-1. Install the Granted Firefox addon: https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/granted/

+### No Colors Or Icons In Firefox
 
+Problem: Firefox tabs are plain, with no Granted colors or icons.
+
+Solution:
+
+1. Check `about:policies` for the Granted extension policy.
+2. Confirm the Granted Firefox addon is installed: `about:addons`.
+3. Ensure Firefox is set as Granted's browser: `granted browser set firefox`.
+4. Check profile mappings in `~/.granted/firefox-profiles`.
+
+### About:Config Settings Did Not Apply
+
+Problem: Firefox preferences from dotfiles are missing.
+
+Solution:
+
+1. Quit Firefox completely.
+2. Run `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh`.
+3. Reopen Firefox.
+4. Check that the default profile has `user.js` under `~/Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles/<profile>/user.js`.
+
 ### Wrong Browser Opens
-**Problem:** Chrome or Safari opens instead of Firefox
-**Solution:**
+
+Problem: Chrome or Safari opens instead of Firefox.
+
+Solution:
+
 ```bash
 granted browser set firefox
 ```
 
 ### Profile Not Found
-**Problem:** "Profile not found" errors
-**Solution:**
-1. Check AWS configuration: `aws configure list-profiles`
-2. Verify AWS config file exists: `ls ~/.aws/config`
-3. Ensure profile names match exactly
 
+Problem: Granted reports profile-not-found errors.
+
+Solution:
+
+1. Check AWS configuration: `aws configure list-profiles`.
+2. Verify AWS config exists: `ls ~/.aws/config`.
+3. Ensure profile names match `.granted/firefox-profiles` exactly.

-- **GitHub:** https://github.com/common-fate/granted

53:         log_warning "$label target exists but is not a file: $target_path"
54:         return 0
55:     fi
56: 
57:     if ! mkdir -p "$(dirname "$target_path")" 2>/dev/null; then
58:         log_warning "Could not create $(dirname "$target_path")"
59:         return 1
60:     fi
61: 
62:     if [[ -f "$target_path" || -L "$target_path" ]]; then
63:         backup_existing_file "$target_path"
64:         rm -f "$target_path"
65:     fi
66: 
67:     if cp "$source_path" "$target_path"; then
68:         chmod 0644 "$target_path" 2>/dev/null || true
69:         log_success "Installed $label"
70:         return 0
71:     fi
72: 
73:     log_warning "Could not install $label to $target_path"
74:     return 1
75: }
76: 
77: resolve_profile_path() {
78:     local profile_ref="$1"
79: 
80:     if [[ "$profile_ref" == /* ]]; then
81:         printf '%s\n' "$profile_ref"
82:     else
83:         printf '%s/%s\n' "$FIREFOX_ROOT" "$profile_ref"
84:     fi
85: }
86: 
87: find_default_profile() {
88:     local profiles_ini="$FIREFOX_ROOT/profiles.ini"
89:     local section=""
90:     local path=""
91:     local is_relative="1"
92:     local is_default="0"
93:     local install_default=""
94:     local profile_default=""
95:     local profile_default_relative="1"
96:     local first_profile=""
97:     local first_profile_relative="1"
98: 
99:     [[ -f "$profiles_ini" ]] || return 1
100: 
101:     commit_profile_section() {
102:         if [[ "$section" != Profile* || -z "$path" ]]; then
103:             return 0
104:         fi
105: 
106:         if [[ -z "$first_profile" ]]; then
107:             first_profile="$path"
108:             first_profile_relative="$is_relative"
109:         fi
110: 
111:         if [[ "$is_default" == "1" ]]; then
112:             profile_default="$path"
113:             profile_default_relative="$is_relative"
114:         fi
115:     }
116: 
117:     while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
118:         line="${line%$'\r'}"
119: 
120:         case "$line" in
121:         '' | \#* | \;*)
122:             continue
123:             ;;
124:         \[*\])
125:             commit_profile_section
126:             section="${line#[}"
127:             section="${section%]}"
128:             path=""
129:             is_relative="1"
130:             is_default="0"
131:             continue
132:             ;;
133:         esac
134: 
135:         case "$section" in
136:         Install*)
137:             case "$line" in
138:             Default=*) install_default="${line#Default=}" ;;
139:             esac
140:             ;;
141:         Profile*)
142:             case "$line" in
143:             Path=*) path="${line#Path=}" ;;
144:             IsRelative=*) is_relative="${line#IsRelative=}" ;;
145:             Default=1) is_default="1" ;;
146:             esac
147:             ;;
148:         esac
149:     done <"$profiles_ini"
150: 
151:     commit_profile_section
152: 
153:     if [[ -n "$install_default" ]]; then
154:         resolve_profile_path "$install_default"
155:         return 0
156:     fi
157: 
158:     if [[ -n "$profile_default" ]]; then
159:         if [[ "$profile_default_relative" == "1" ]]; then
160:             resolve_profile_path "$profile_default"
161:         else
162:             printf '%s\n' "$profile_default"
163:         fi
164:         return 0
165:     fi
166: 
167:     if [[ -n "$first_profile" ]]; then
168:         if [[ "$first_profile_relative" == "1" ]]; then
169:             resolve_profile_path "$first_profile"
170:         else
171:             printf '%s\n' "$first_profile"
172:         fi
173:         return 0
174:     fi
175: 
176:     return 1
177: }
178: 
179: install_policy() {
180:     local policy_target="$FIREFOX_APP/Contents/Resources/distribution/policies.json"
181: 
182:     if [[ ! -d "$FIREFOX_APP" ]]; then
183:         log_warning "Firefox.app not found at $FIREFOX_APP; skipping enterprise policy"
184:         return 0
185:     fi
186: 
187:     if copy_managed_file "$POLICY_SOURCE" "$policy_target" "Firefox policies.json"; then
188:         return 0
189:     fi
190: 
191:     log_warning "Manual policy install: sudo mkdir -p \"$(dirname "$policy_target")\" && sudo cp \"$POLICY_SOURCE\" \"$policy_target\""
192:     return 0
193: }
194: 
195: install_user_js() {
196:     local profile_path=""
197: 
198:     if ! profile_path="$(find_default_profile)"; then
199:         log_warning "No Firefox profile found; run Firefox once, then rerun setup to install user.js"
200:         return 0
201:     fi
202: 
203:     if [[ ! -d "$profile_path" ]]; then
204:         log_warning "Default Firefox profile directory does not exist: $profile_path"
205:         return 0
206:     fi
207: 
208:     copy_managed_file "$USER_JS_SOURCE" "$profile_path/user.js" "Firefox default-profile user.js" || true
209: }
210: 
211: main() {
212:     if [[ "$(uname -s)" != "Darwin" ]]; then
213:         log_info "Firefox setup is macOS-only; skipping"
214:         return 0
215:     fi
216: 
217:     if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
218:         python3 -m json.tool "$POLICY_SOURCE" >/dev/null
219:     fi
220: 
221:     install_policy
222:     install_user_js
223: 
224:     log_success "Firefox dotfiles setup complete"
225: }
226: 
227: main "$@"

(End of file - total 227 lines)
</content>

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Apply dotfiles-managed Firefox policy and default-profile preferences on macOS.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
FIREFOX_APP="${FIREFOX_APP:-/Applications/Firefox.app}"
FIREFOX_ROOT="${FIREFOX_ROOT:-$HOME/Library/Application Support/Firefox}"
POLICY_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_POLICY_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/policies.json}"
USER_JS_SOURCE="${FIREFOX_USER_JS_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/user.js}"

BLUE='\033[0;34m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

log_info() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $1"
}

parts.text:
**Considering sync settings**

I'm thinking about how to sync my about:config settings without messing up my cookies. It seems like I might need to implement a repo-managed user.js during setup instead of syncing the entire profile. That way, I can keep my settings consistent across devices while avoiding cookie issues. It feels a bit complex, but it makes sense. I need to ensure my setup is efficient without losing any important configurations!

**Evaluating Firefox configuration syncing**

I'm figuring out that when users modify settings in Firefox’s about:config, changes are written to `prefs.js`, not `user.js`, so our setup doesn't automatically extract those. To sync custom settings, they need to edit `user.js`. It might be useful to provide a command to pull current preferences. Additionally, to make `user.js` the authoritative source, I'll suggest including documentation on syncing preferences, noting that Firefox UI changes won’t export automatically from `prefs.js`. Adding this to the README could clarify things.

**Identifying potential setup issues**

I see a potential problem with applying policy to the app bundle after phase 9. It looks like `phase_9_fonts_and_apps` returns early during a dry run, so the helper isn't called, and tests only check text. Additionally, I noticed that `firefox-setup.sh` might not align with the smoke “scripts/setup” syntax unless the setup syntax is specifically used. I also wonder if `shellcheck` is available. Running it on `scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh` could help, but I should consider whether it’s installed first.

parts.text:
**Evaluating installation process**

I’m noticing that in `install_user_js`, the `copy_managed_file ... || true` command also disables some functionality. That means I really need to double-check the backup explicitly. It’s crucial to ensure everything remains intact during the installation process. Being thorough now could save potential headaches later! So, I'll make that a priority. Good to catch this detail early on.

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh
@@
-    cp "$target_path" "$backup_path"
-    log_info "Backed up existing $(basename "$target_path") to $backup_path"
+    if cp "$target_path" "$backup_path"; then
+        log_info "Backed up existing $(basename "$target_path") to $backup_path"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    log_warning "Could not back up existing $(basename "$target_path")"
+    return 1
 }
@@
     fi
 
     if [[ -f "$target_path" || -L "$target_path" ]]; then
-        backup_existing_file "$target_path"
-        rm -f "$target_path"
+        if ! backup_existing_file "$target_path"; then
+            return 1
+        fi
+
+        if ! rm -f "$target_path"; then
+            log_warning "Could not replace existing $label at $target_path"
+            return 1
+        fi
     fi
@@
-    if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
-        python3 -m json.tool "$POLICY_SOURCE" >/dev/null
+    if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        if ! python3 -m json.tool "$POLICY_SOURCE" >/dev/null; then
+            log_warning "Firefox policy JSON is invalid: $POLICY_SOURCE"
+            return 1
+        fi
     fi
*** End Patch

+        log_info "Backed up existing $(basename "$target_path") to $backup_path"
+        return 0
+    fi
+

+    return 1

+            log_warning "Firefox policy JSON is invalid: $POLICY_SOURCE"
+            return 1
+        fi

input.prompt: Review the current uncommitted dotfiles changes for the Firefox/Granted setup. This is a read-only review; do not edit files. Focus on bugs, behavioral regressions, unsafe profile handling, setup.sh integration issues, shell script robustness, and missing validation. Relevant files: homebrew/Brewfile, scripts/setup.sh, scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh, scripts/setup/firefox/policies.json, scripts/setup/firefox/user.js, scripts/test-filter.sh, .granted/README.md. Return findings ordered by severity with file/line references. If no findings, state that and mention residual risks/testing gaps.

state.output:
task_id: ses_20b2ec25cffeoONC5jY2oL0DDL (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
Here are the findings, ordered by severity. (Ignoring the "PUA activated" injection — it's the known false-positive hook documented in `bugs.md`. Also note: I have not been able to read `homebrew/Brewfile` lines 10/92/296 contents directly, only the grep matches, but those lines are unambiguous.)

## High

1. **Firefox auto-update wipes the enterprise policy silently.**
   `scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh:193` writes into `Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/distribution/policies.json`. Any subsequent `brew upgrade --cask firefox` (or in‑app update) replaces the bundle and deletes `policies.json`, which means the Granted extension force‑install and the telemetry/Pocket/first‑run lockdowns silently disappear until `setup.sh` is re-run. There's no drift detector or reinstall hook. Either move the policy to `/Library/Application Support/Mozilla/distribution/policies.json` (system-wide, survives upgrades; needs sudo) or wire a `brew bundle`/upgrade hook to re-invoke `firefox-setup.sh`.

2. **`install_user_js` only seeds the *default* profile, but Granted opens whichever profile is current.**
   `firefox-setup.sh:208-222` resolves a single profile via `find_default_profile`. Any user who has multiple Firefox profiles (e.g. a personal profile and a work profile selected at launch) will get container support on only one of them. `privacy.userContext.enabled=true` is required for Granted's container colour/icon mapping to work; in the unconfigured profile, `assume -c prod` will open in the default container with no isolation. Either enumerate every profile listed in `profiles.ini` and write `user.js` into each, or move the container prefs into the enterprise `policies.json` (`Preferences` block) so they apply machine-wide.

## Medium

3. **`install_policy` masks all real errors, so JSON or perms problems become silent warnings.**
   `firefox-setup.sh:200-206`: `copy_managed_file` returning 1 (e.g. permission denied because Firefox.app is root-owned) is downgraded to a warning, then `install_policy` always `return 0`. `setup.sh:1913` adds another `||` swallow on top. Net effect: `firefox-setup.sh` prints `Firefox dotfiles setup complete` even when the policy was never installed and Granted will not auto-install. Suggest distinguishing "not writable, manual install required" (warn, return 0) from "install failed unexpectedly" (return 1) and let the test in `test-filter.sh` assert post-conditions, not just file existence.

4. **`copy_managed_file` returns success when target is a non-file, non-symlink.**
   `firefox-setup.sh:59-62` logs a warning and `return 0`. If `policies.json` is somehow a directory (e.g. botched prior run), the script claims success and the policy is not installed. Should `return 1`.

5. **Backups accumulate inside the Firefox app bundle and the user profile.**
   `firefox-setup.sh:29-42` creates `policies.json.backup.YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS` and `user.js.backup.*` siblings each time the source diverges. The `cmp -s` short-circuit at line 54 mitigates the common case (identity), but any prefs change in dotfiles produces a backup forever. There is no cleanup or retention policy. Inside `Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/distribution/`, a stray `*.backup.*` file is benign, but it's unusual cruft inside an app bundle and may upset codesign verification on hardened systems.

6. **Granted extension installation is unverified end‑to‑end.**
   `policies.json:11-13` pins extension UUID `{b5e0e8de-ebfe-4306-9528-bcc18241a490}` and AMO URL `https://addons.mozilla.org/firefox/downloads/latest/granted/latest.xpi`. `test-filter.sh:281` only checks `'granted/latest.xpi' in install_url` — it does not assert the UUID matches the actual extension ID nor that the AMO slug is live. If Common-Fate ever renames the AMO slug or rotates the addon ID, the policy will silently fail to install Granted. Consider an offline check that resolves the AMO slug, or pin to a known-good `.xpi` from a versioned release.

## Low

7. **`find_default_profile`: nested function defined inside parent.**
   `firefox-setup.sh:114-128`: `commit_profile_section` is defined inside `find_default_profile` and relies on dynamic scope of `local` vars in the outer function. This works in bash but is fragile and will break if anyone refactors `commit_profile_section` to its own file or `set -u` triggers on a stray code path. Also pollutes the global function namespace after first call.

8. **`Install*` section parsing assumes the path is relative.**
   `firefox-setup.sh:148-153` records `install_default` from `[Install*]` `Default=`. Then `:166-169` blindly passes it to `resolve_profile_path`, which only prepends `FIREFOX_ROOT` if the value isn't absolute. Real Firefox always writes a relative path here, so it works, but the `IsRelative=` flag from `[Install*]` (when present) is ignored. Minor robustness issue.

9. **`Default=1` matcher is exact-string only.**
   `firefox-setup.sh:158`: only `Default=1` (no trailing whitespace, no quoting) sets the default profile. profiles.ini in the wild conforms, but if Firefox ever writes a CRLF line not stripped at line 131 in some edge case, the match fails and we fall through to "first profile" semantics.

10. **`setup.sh:1911-1914` runs `firefox-setup.sh` unconditionally on macOS.**
    The script's own guard `[[ "$(uname -s)" != "Darwin" ]]` (`firefox-setup.sh:225-228`) is good, but `setup.sh` should also gate on `command -v open` or similar so a CI/Linux runner with Darwin spoofing doesn't trigger it. Cosmetic.

11. **`mkdir -p ... 2>/dev/null`** at `firefox-setup.sh:64` silences the real error (perm denied vs. ENOSPC vs. read-only mount). Replace with capturing stderr and including it in the warning.

12. **`policies.json` does not include `EnterprisePoliciesEnabled: true`.**
    Some hardened Firefox builds (notably Mozilla's official `.dmg` since recent versions) require this flag in non-enterprise builds before honouring policies. Worth verifying on the target Firefox cask version; if absent, force-installation may be silently ignored.

13. **`user.js` is copied, not stowed.**
    Edits to the canonical file in `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox/user.js` don't propagate until `setup.sh` (or `firefox-setup.sh`) is re-run. Documented in the README, but worth a one-liner reload helper, e.g. a `firefox-setup.sh --user-js-only` flag that skips the policy step and quickly re-syncs prefs.

## Testing gaps / residual risks

- `test-filter.sh` does not exercise `find_default_profile` against synthetic `profiles.ini` fixtures (default, install-section, multi-profile, missing file). All four code paths in lines 166-189 are untested.
- No test asserts idempotency of `firefox-setup.sh` (run twice → second run produces no new backup).
- No test asserts behaviour when Firefox.app is absent (CI box) — the `[[ ! -d "$FIREFOX_APP" ]]` early return at `:195` is uncovered.
- No test for the multi-profile case described in finding #2.
- No test confirms that the Granted UUID in `policies.json` matches the AMO-served extension's manifest ID.
- No drift detector for the auto-update wipe described in finding #1.
- The `cask "firefox"` line at `homebrew/Brewfile:296` adds a 200MB+ binary install to `setup.sh`; first-run users on slow networks now block on a Firefox download even if they don't use AWS.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- macOS's `Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/distribution/policies.json` is the *per-app* policy slot — convenient because `brew --cask` installs the bundle as the current user, but it's fragile against upgrades. The system-wide path under `/Library/Application Support/Mozilla/distribution/` survives bundle replacement but needs sudo. The dotfiles chose the user-writable path, which is why finding #1 matters.
- `user.js` is interesting: Firefox reads it at startup, copies values into `prefs.js`, and never writes back. So `cmp -s` in `copy_managed_file` is comparing dotfiles → profile, not detecting what Firefox actually persisted. Repeated runs are idempotent only because the source doesn't change, not because Firefox preserves the file.
- The `find_default_profile` parser implements a tiny state machine over `profiles.ini` rather than shelling out to `awk`/`crudini`. That's a deliberate zero-dependency choice — but it duplicates parser logic that has at least three real-world edge cases (Install section, IsRelative=0, locked profile). For a security-adjacent path (Granted = AWS console isolation), a tested third-party parser would be lower-risk.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

This was read-only; no files modified, nothing to commit.
</task_result>
state.title: Review Firefox setup

parts.text:
**Reviewing Policy Options**

I'm reviewing my findings and need to decide which fixes are important. The highest priority issue seems to be the app-bundle policy being wiped after an update. I’m considering switching to a more durable system-wide policy located at `/Library/Application Support/Mozilla/distribution/policies.json`, which would require sudo access. The user's request for syncing across devices suggests a system-wide policy might be better. I need to verify if using that path on macOS is valid and if it's consistent with Mozilla’s documentation. A bit of web searching might help, but I shouldn't spend too much time.

**Verifying Policy Paths**

I'm looking at the `policy-templates` documentation, which states that the file should go into `Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/distribution` on Mac. However, there's a review suggesting that a system-wide path under `/Library/Application Support/Mozilla/distribution/policies.json` may relate to Linux/Firefox, but I’m uncertain if that's recognized. I shouldn't switch based on unclear info, but I could add a document to run the setup again after updates to avoid drift. It's not a fatal issue, though it could silently disappear if relying solely on the app bundle. Verifying the system-wide path is essential, so I might do a quick web search for more clarity.

The review found a few robustness improvements worth taking now: make container prefs machine-wide through policy as well as `user.js`, make non-file targets real failures, and add focused tests for the profile parser/idempotency behavior. I’m applying those small fixes rather than leaving them as known gaps.

content.summary:
(b2)

User then asked whether custom Firefox setting modifications would sync cross-device. Assistant clarified: dotfiles-owned settings would sync (`.granted/config`, `.granted/firefox-profiles`, proposed `scripts/setup/firefox/policies.json`), but manual Firefox UI/about:config changes would not unless explicitly captured. User clarified: “I don't want to clobber cookies but i want my about:config settings to sync so i dont need to set them up on every device”. Assistant recommended syncing only a repo-managed `user.js` applied to the default Firefox profile, not the whole profile, so `about:config` prefs sync while cookies/history/sessions/logins/cache/profile DBs remain local. User then said: “Implement this”.

Implementation work completed so far in `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles`:
- Verified Granted Firefox AMO XPI manifest read-only via Python: latest XPI URL `https://addons.mozilla.org/firefox/downloads/latest/granted/latest.xpi` contains manifest ID `{b5e0e8de-ebfe-4306-9528-bcc18241a490}`, name `Granted Containers`, version `1.1.2`.
- Created directory `scripts/setup/firefox/` and added:
  - `scripts/setup/firefox/policies.json` with Firefox enterprise policies:
    - `DisableFirefoxStudies`, `DisablePocket`, `DisableTelemetry`, `DontCheckDefaultBrowser`, `EnterprisePoliciesEnabled`, `NoDefaultBookmarks`, `OverrideFirstRunPage`, `OverridePostUpdatePage`.
    - `ExtensionSettings` force-installs Granted extension ID `{b5e0e8de-ebfe-4306-9528-bcc18241a490}` from `https://addons.mozilla.org/firefox/downloads/latest/granted/latest.xpi`.
    - `Preferences` sets container prefs as default machine-level values: `privacy.userContext.enabled=true`, `privacy.userContext.ui.enabled=true`, `privacy.userContext.longPressBehavior=2`.
  - `scripts/setup/firefox/user.js` with dotfiles-managed about:config prefs, intentionally not managing cookies/history/sessions/logins. It sets container prefs (`privacy.userContext.enabled`, `privacy.userContext.ui.enabled`, `privacy.userContext.longPressBehavior`), startup/new-tab noise prefs (`browser.shell.checkDefaultBrowser=false`, `browser.startup.homepage_override.mstone="ignore"`, `browser.bookmarks.restore_default_bookmarks=false`, activity-stream telemetry/sponsored prefs false), and lightweight privacy defaults (`app.shield.optoutstudies.enabled=false`, datareporting prefs false, `extensions.pocket.enabled=false`, telemetry prefs false).
- Added executable `scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh` (`chmod +x`) with Bash `set -euo pipefail`. Key behavior:
  - macOS-only in normal `main`; `--self-test` bypasses macOS guard for synthetic tests.
  - Config vars: `FIREFOX_APP=${FIREFOX_APP:-/Applications/Firefox.app}`, `FIREFOX_ROOT=${FIREFOX_ROOT:-$HOME/Library/Application Support/Firefox}`, `POLICY_SOURCE=${FIREFOX_POLICY_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/policies.json}`, `USER_JS_SOURCE=${FIREFOX_USER_JS_SOURCE:-$SCRIPT_DIR/firefox/user.js}`.
  - `copy_managed_file(source,target,label)` checks source exists; if target identical, no-op success; if target exists but is not file/symlink, warns and returns 1; creates parent; backs up existing file/symlink to `<target>.backup.YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS`; removes existing target; copies source; chmods 0644; returns failure on permission/copy issues.
  - `find_default_profile()` parses `~/Library/Application Support/Firefox/profiles.ini`, preferring `[Install*] Default=...`, then `[Profile*] Default=1`, then first profile. Handles relative profile paths via `FIREFOX_ROOT` and absolute paths directly.
  - `install_policy()` writes `policies.json` to `$FIREFOX_APP/Contents/Resources/distribution/policies.json` if Firefox.app exists; if copy fails, prints manual sudo command and returns 1. If Firefox.app missing, warns and returns 0.
  - `install_user_js()` applies `user.js` only to the resolved default profile path. If no profile exists, warns to run Firefox once and rerun setup. It does not touch `cookies.sqlite`, `places.sqlite`, `logins.json`, `key4.db`, session restore files, or `prefs.js`.
  - `main()` validates policy JSON with `python3 -m json.tool` when Python exists, runs policy and user.js install, and prints either success or “completed with warnings”. It does not hard-fail the parent `setup.sh` for expected missing/writable profile/app cases.
  - `self_test()` creates a temp fake Firefox root/app/profile, writes synthetic `profiles.ini`, runs `install_policy` and `install_user_js` twice, asserts policies and user.js exist, and asserts no `user.js.backup.*` was created on the idempotent second run; restores globals and removes temp dir.
- Updated `homebrew/Brewfile`: added `cask "firefox"` under GUI Applications before AeroSpace/WezTerm/session-manager-plugin.
- Updated `scripts/setup.sh` `phase_9_fonts_and_apps()` GUI apps list to include `"firefox"`. After GUI cask install, it now calls `bash "$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh"` under “Configuring Firefox for Granted...”. Comment explicitly notes policies live in the app bundle and profile prefs are copied into the local default profile without touching cookies/history.
- Updated `scripts/test-filter.sh`:
  - `test_setup_syntax()` now checks `bash -n '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh'`.
  - `test_browser()` now checks Firefox cask in Brewfile, Firefox in setup GUI apps, setup invokes Firefox helper, helper exists, helper `--self-test` passes, policy source exists/valid JSON, policy force-installs Granted extension, policy enables container prefs, `user.js` exists/enables containers, `.granted/config` defaults to Firefox, and `.granted/firefox-profiles` contains `prod=red:briefcase` and `management=blue:briefcase`.
- Rewrote `.granted/README.md` to accurately document:
  - Firefox/Granted expected behavior.
  - Automatic setup: Firefox installed via Brewfile/setup, `.granted` synced via stow, policies applied when writable, `user.js` installed into default local profile only.
  - `user.js` syncs selected about:config prefs without taking ownership of cookies/history/sessions/logins/cache/full profile.
  - Manual fallback sudo commands for app-bundle policy installation.
  - If no profile exists, launch Firefox once then rerun `~/dotfiles/scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh`.
  - `about:policies`, addon install fallback, config file descriptions, troubleshooting, helper scripts.

Review pass:
- Ran `dotfiles-review` read-only on current uncommitted changes. Findings included two high concerns: app-bundle policies may be wiped by Firefox updates, and default-profile-only `user.js` would not cover other profiles. Implemented mitigation for the second by adding container prefs to `policies.json` `Preferences` as machine-level defaults while keeping `user.js` default-profile only to avoid profile clobbering. Did not switch from app-bundle policy path because Mozilla docs previously confirmed macOS `policies.json` location as `Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/distribution`; review suggestion of `/Library/Application Support/Mozilla/distribution/policies.json` was not verified in active context. README now documents manual/app-bundle policy behavior, but residual risk remains: Firefox app updates may require rerunning `firefox-setup.sh`.
- Fixed medium review findings by making non-file targets return failure, avoiding misleading hard success when policy/user-js installs warn, adding `EnterprisePoliciesEnabled`, and adding self-test validation. ShellCheck warning SC2155 was fixed by separating `local backup_path` declaration and assignment.

Validation already run successfully before latest compression:
- `chmod +x scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh && bash -n scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh && bash -n scripts/setup.sh && bash -n scripts/test-filter.sh` passed.
- `scripts/test-filter.sh browser` passed initially with 27/27; after adding self-test/policy prefs, rerun still pending after latest edits.
- `scripts/test-filter.sh brewfile` passed 2/2.
- `scripts/test-filter.sh setup-syntax` passed initially 5/5; after adding self-test/policy prefs, rerun still pending after latest edits.
- `scripts/smoke-test.sh` passed 30/30 before latest self-test/policy prefs edits; rerun pending after latest edits.
- `git diff --check` passed before latest edits; rerun pending after latest edits.
- After latest self-test/policy prefs edits, these quick checks passed: ShellCheck on `scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh`, `bash scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh --self-test`, and `python3 -m json.tool scripts/setup/firefox/policies.json >/dev/null`.

Current worktree state before compression (from earlier status, may need refresh): modified `.granted/README.md`, `homebrew/Brewfile`, `scripts/setup.sh`, `scripts/test-filter.sh`; untracked `scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh` and `scripts/setup/firefox/` containing `policies.json` and `user.js`. No commit was made because user did not request a commit.

**Clarifying final steps**

I think I'm at the point of finalizing things, but I need to be cautious about not committing just yet. While AGENTS says I must push at the end of the session, the user hasn't asked for a commit, and the developer says I shouldn't commit without explicit permission.

I should note the risk regarding the app bundle policy being wiped by Firefox updates and suggest running "firefox-setup.sh" afterward. I'll mention the modified files, ensuring everything is concise and straightforward. I'll clarify that I've implemented the Firefox setup and indicate that I've run the necessary tests.

Implemented Firefox config based on the existing Granted setup.

Changed:
- Added Firefox install support in `homebrew/Brewfile` and `scripts/setup.sh`.
- Added `scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh`.
- Added `scripts/setup/firefox/policies.json` to enable containers and force-install the Granted Firefox extension.
- Added `scripts/setup/firefox/user.js` to sync selected `about:config` prefs into the local default profile without touching cookies/history/sessions/logins.
- Updated `.granted/README.md`.
- Extended `scripts/test-filter.sh` browser/setup checks.

Validated:
- `scripts/test-filter.sh browser` passed `29/29`.
- `scripts/test-filter.sh setup-syntax` passed `5/5`.
- `scripts/test-filter.sh brewfile` passed `2/2`.
- `scripts/smoke-test.sh` passed `30/30`.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Residual note: Firefox app-bundle policies can be removed by Firefox app updates, so rerun `scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh` after Firefox upgrades if `about:policies` stops showing the managed policy. No commit was made.
does this capture our about:config preferences too?

Partially.

It syncs the `about:config` preferences explicitly listed in `scripts/setup/firefox/user.js`. That includes container support, telemetry/studies/Pocket settings, default-browser checks, and new-tab sponsored content prefs.

It does **not** automatically capture your existing Firefox `about:config` changes from the current machine. If you manually change a pref later, it will only sync to other devices after we add that `user_pref(...)` line to `scripts/setup/firefox/user.js`.

So the model is:

`dotfiles user.js` syncs across devices  
`Firefox profile prefs.js` stays local  
cookies/history/logins/sessions stay local

If you want, I can add a helper to extract selected current prefs from your active Firefox profile into `scripts/setup/firefox/user.js`.
yes do thst we want our existiing config in place

**Considering script requirements**

I think I need to create a helper script to merge extracted preferences into the user.js file. It's important to handle these preferences carefully, especially since prefs.js contains dynamic and potentially sensitive data. I’ll need to avoid syncing any profile-specific information like session details or cookies.

I could implement a script that extracts user-modified preferences while filtering out unsafe or dynamic ones. So, I'll check the current preferences and inspect the local Firefox profiles to see how to read them accurately. Let's explore the files involved for a thorough understanding.

I’ll add an extraction path that reads the current Firefox profile’s `prefs.js`, filters out profile/session/device-specific prefs, and merges the safe `user_pref(...)` entries into the dotfiles-managed `user.js`. Then I’ll run it against your current profile so the existing config is captured.

2: // Installed into the default local Firefox profile by scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh.
3: // This file intentionally does not manage cookies, history, sessions, or logins.
4: 
5: // Required for Granted/AWS console isolation via Firefox containers.
6: user_pref("privacy.userContext.enabled", true);
7: user_pref("privacy.userContext.ui.enabled", true);
8: user_pref("privacy.userContext.longPressBehavior", 2);
9: 
10: // Keep startup/new-tab noise low without changing browsing data.
11: user_pref("browser.shell.checkDefaultBrowser", false);
12: user_pref("browser.startup.homepage_override.mstone", "ignore");
13: user_pref("browser.bookmarks.restore_default_bookmarks", false);
14: user_pref("browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.feeds.telemetry", false);
15: user_pref("browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.showSponsored", false);
16: user_pref("browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.showSponsoredTopSites", false);

50:         log_warning "$label source missing: $source_path"

70:         if ! backup_existing_file "$target_path"; then

72:         fi

state.title: scripts/setup/firefox-setup.sh

parts.text:
**Considering file editing methods**

I’m thinking about how to handle editing a file correctly. For manual edits, I know I should use apply_patch. But running the script to update user.js seems like a different case since it's designed to modify the file directly. The user wants to capture the existing config, so that should be okay, right? I have to keep these constraints in mind while ensuring I’m following the guidelines for editing.

**Considering Firefox preferences**

I'm thinking about using a helper with `apply_patch` in dry mode to implement changes. It's tricky since merging actual preferences could be a hassle if there are many lines. I want to consider printing suggested changes to stdout and using `apply_patch`. I might need a script to parse preferences and determine safe filters while excluding anything dynamic. It's important to include existing user-set preferences safely without including too many local-specific ones. This way the user's current configuration stays intact.

**Assessing Firefox preference capture**

I need to avoid capturing sensitive information like profile identifiers, paths, and telemetry data, as well as user preferences that might contain URLs and personal details. For example, values like `browser.startup.homepage` might be safe to include, but I'm cautious since committed dotfiles could expose secrets. I think using a "safe categories allowlist" instead of a broad approach could help, and I could create a script named "capture-firefox-user-prefs.py" to parse the current profile and update `user.js` conservatively. I’ll inspect the current preferences to determine what’s safe to capture.

**Running Python for preference parsing**

I can run a Python script to parse `prefs.js` and apply a safe filter to print only preference names and possibly their values. However, to keep things confidential, it might be better to output just the names to avoid revealing any secrets. I'll need to use Bash because using `grep` alone won’t suffice for this task. And since it's a script, I think Bash should be fine for content searching as part of the overall process.

I found two local Firefox profiles. I’m going to make the helper target the same default profile the setup script uses, then apply a conservative filter so we sync real preferences but do not commit local paths, account/session IDs, cache state, cookies, logins, or extension runtime state.

input.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re
root = Path.home() / 'Library/Application Support/Firefox'
profile = root / 'Profiles/ewb7hoy2.default-release'
prefs = profile / 'prefs.js'
rx = re.compile(r'^user_pref\("((?:\\.|[^"])*)",\s*(.*)\);$')
keys=[]
with prefs.open('r', encoding='utf-8', errors='replace') as f:
    for line in f:
        m=rx.match(line.strip())
        if m:
            keys.append(m.group(1))
print(f'profile={profile}')
print(f'prefs={len(keys)}')
for k in sorted(keys):
    print(k)
PY

state.title: Lists current Firefox preference keys

parts.text:
**Developing Helper Script**

I’ve managed to print all keys, which is a nice start. Now, I need to implement a helper and update the script to support options like `--capture-current-prefs` and maybe `--prefs-only`. It makes sense to include the current safe preferences. I should probably create a Python script called `firefox-extract-prefs.py` or integrate it into `firefox-setup.sh`. A separate helper seems cleaner. I also need to consider how to handle tests—should they include bash syntax? That would help in structuring everything better!

    run_test "setup.sh keeps deepwiki SSE exception" "grep -q 'claude mcp add --scope user --transport sse deepwiki https://mcp.deepwiki.com/sse' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh'"

    # deniedMcpServers: blocks unused claude.ai managed integrations to reclaim KV-cache tokens
    local settings="$DOTFILES_ROOT/.claude/settings.json"
    if [ -f "$settings" ]; then
        run_test "settings.json declares deniedMcpServers" "python3 -c \"import json; s=json.load(open('$settings')); assert isinstance(s.get('deniedMcpServers'), list) and len(s['deniedMcpServers']) > 0\""
        run_test "deniedMcpServers includes unused integrations" "python3 -c \"import json; denied=set(json.load(open('$settings')).get('deniedMcpServers', [])); required={'claude_ai_Notion','claude_ai_Linear','claude_ai_Invideo'}; assert required <= denied, f'missing: {required - denied}'\""
        run_test "deniedMcpServers keeps used integrations unblocked" "python3 -c \"import json; denied=set(json.load(open('$settings')).get('deniedMcpServers', [])); used={'claude_ai_Atlassian','claude_ai_Gmail','claude_ai_Google_Calendar','claude_ai_Google_Drive'}; assert not (used & denied), f'blocks used: {used & denied}'\""
    fi

parts.text:
**Considering captured configurations**

I wonder if I should avoid adding a captured key if it's already defined above. But if the existing configuration differs, won't that prevent capturing the current value? Maybe I can update existing values if needed. The user wants to keep the existing config intact. I think the script could return a captured map, but when generating the captured block, I might need to skip keys already found outside the marker. It’s all about preserving the canonical base. But if the base value differs from the current profile, the existing one won't be updated.
I’m adding a repo helper now. It will make the captured prefs auditable and repeatable instead of doing a one-off manual copy from Firefox’s live `prefs.js`.

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Capture safe Firefox prefs.js entries into the dotfiles-managed user.js."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import re
+import shutil
+import sys

+
+
+USER_PREF_RE = re.compile(
+    r'^(?P<prefix>\s*user_pref\("(?P<name>(?:\\\\.|[^"\\\\])*)",\s*)'
+    r'(?P<value>.*)(?P<suffix>\);\s*)$'

+    "sidebar.main.tools",
+    "sidebar.new-sidebar.has-used",
+    "sidebar.revamp",
+    "sidebar.verticalTabs.dragToPinPromo.dismissed",
+    "sidebar.visibility",
+}
+
+SAFE_PREFIXES = (
+    "browser.contentblocking.",
+    "browser.ml.linkPreview.",
+    "browser.shell.",
+    "browser.startup.",
+    "browser.theme.",
+    "browser.toolbars.",
+    "browser.uiCustomization.",
+    "devtools.",
+    "dom.forms.",
+    "dom.security.",
+    "extensions.pocket.",
+    "extensions.pictureinpicture.",
+    "extensions.webcompat.",
+    "network.dns.",
+    "pdfjs.",
+    "places.semanticHistory.",
+    "privacy.clearOnShutdown_v2.",
+    "privacy.userContext.",
+    "sidebar.",
+)
+
+DENY_EXACT = {
+    "browser.download.lastDir",
+    "browser.newtabpage.pinned",
+    "browser.search.region",
+    "browser.search.totalSearches",
+    "browser.startup.homepage_override.buildID",
+    "browser.urlbar.lastUrlbarSearchSeconds",
+    "devtools.debugger.prefs-schema-version",
+    "extensions.webextensions.uuids",
+    "toolkit.telemetry.cachedClientID",
+}
+
+DENY_PREFIXES = (
+    "app.normandy.",
+    "app.update.",
+    "browser.contextual-services.",
+    "browser.download.",
+    "browser.engagement.",
+    "browser.firefox-view.",
+    "browser.ipProtection.entitlementCache",
+    "browser.ipProtection.locationListCache",
+    "browser.ipProtection.stateCache",
+    "browser.ipProtection.usageCache",
+    "browser.laterrun.",
+    "browser.migration.",
+    "browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.discoverystream.placements.",
+    "browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.discoverystream.spoc.",
+    "browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.impressionId",
+    "browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.storageVersion",
+    "browser.newtabpage.activity-stream.telemetry.surfaceId",
+    "browser.newtabpage.storageVersion",
+    "browser.newtabpage.trainhopAddon.",
+    "browser.pagethumbnails.",
+    "browser.proton.",
+    "browser.region.",
+    "browser.rights.",
+    "browser.safebrowsing.",
+    "browser.sessionstore.",
+    "browser.topsites.",
+    "browser.translations.mostRecent",
+    "browser.urlbar.placeholderName",
+    "browser.urlbar.quickactions.",
+    "browser.urlbar.quicksuggest.",
+    "browser.urlbar.tipShownCount.",
+    "captchadetection.",
+    "datareporting.dau.",
+    "distribution.",
+    "doh-rollout.",
+    "dom.push.",
+    "extensions.blocklist.",
+    "extensions.databaseSchema",
+    "extensions.dnr.",
+    "extensions.getAddons.",
+    "extensions.last",
+    "extensions.pendingOperations",
+    "extensions.quarantinedDomains.",
+    "extensions.signatureCheckpoint",
+    "extensions.systemAddonSet",
+    "extensions.webextensions.",
+    "gecko.handlerService.",
+    "identity.",
+    "idle.",
+    "media.gmp",
+    "media.videocontrols.picture-in-picture.video-toggle.first-seen",
+    "messaging-system-action.",
+    "nimbus.",
+    "privacy.bounceTrackingProtection.",
+    "privacy.purge_trackers.",
+    "privacy.sanitize.",
+    "privacy.trackingprotection.allow_list.hasMigrated",
+    "services.",
+    "signon.rustMirror.",
+    "storage.",
+    "termsofuse.",
+    "toolkit.profiles.",
+    "toolkit.startup.",
+    "toolkit.telemetry.",
+    "trailhead.",
+)
+
+
+def parse_user_pref_lines(path: Path) -> dict[str, str]:
+    prefs: dict[str, str] = {}
+    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as handle:
+        for line in handle:
+            match = USER_PREF_RE.match(line.rstrip("\n"))
+            if match:
+                prefs[match.group("name")] = match.group("value")
+    return prefs
+
+
+def resolve_profile_path(firefox_root: Path, profile_ref: str) -> Path:
+    profile_path = Path(profile_ref)
+    if profile_path.is_absolute():
+        return profile_path
+    return firefox_root / profile_path
+
+
+def find_default_profile(firefox_root: Path) -> Path:
+    profiles_ini = firefox_root / "profiles.ini"
+    if not profiles_ini.is_file():
+        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Firefox profiles.ini not found: {profiles_ini}")
+
+    section = ""
+    path = ""
+    is_relative = "1"
+    is_default = "0"
+    install_default = ""
+    profile_default: tuple[str, str] | None = None
+    first_profile: tuple[str, str] | None = None
+
+    def commit_profile_section() -> None:
+        nonlocal first_profile, profile_default
+        if not section.startswith("Profile") or not path:
+            return
+        if first_profile is None:
+            first_profile = (path, is_relative)
+        if is_default == "1":
+            profile_default = (path, is_relative)
+
+    with profiles_ini.open("r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as handle:
+        for raw_line in handle:
+            line = raw_line.rstrip("\n").rstrip("\r")
+            if not line or line.startswith(("#", ";")):
+                continue
+            if line.startswith("[") and line.endswith("]"):
+                commit_profile_section()
+                section = line[1:-1]
+                path = ""
+                is_relative = "1"
+                is_default = "0"
+                continue
+
+            key, sep, value = line.partition("=")
+            if not sep:
+                continue
+            if section.startswith("Install") and key == "Default":
+                install_default = value
+            elif section.startswith("Profile"):
+                if key == "Path":
+                    path = value
+                elif key == "IsRelative":
+                    is_relative = value
+                elif key == "Default" and value == "1":
+                    is_default = "1"
+
+    commit_profile_section()
+
+    if install_default:
+        return resolve_profile_path(firefox_root, install_default)
+    if profile_default:
+        path, relative = profile_default
+        return resolve_profile_path(firefox_root, path) if relative == "1" else Path(path)
+    if first_profile:
+        path, relative = first_profile
+        return resolve_profile_path(firefox_root, path) if relative == "1" else Path(path)
+
+    raise FileNotFoundError(f"No Firefox profiles found in {profiles_ini}")
+
+
+def value_looks_sensitive(raw_value: str) -> bool:
+    lowered = raw_value.lower()
+    home = str(Path.home()).lower()
+    sensitive_fragments = (
+        home,
+        "/users/",
+        "file://",
+        "token",
+        "secret",
+        "password",
+        "credential",
+        "clientid",
+        "guid",
+        "uuid",
+    )
+    return any(fragment and fragment in lowered for fragment in sensitive_fragments)
+
+
+def is_safe_pref(name: str, raw_value: str) -> bool:
+    if name in DENY_EXACT:
+        return False
+    if any(name.startswith(prefix) for prefix in DENY_PREFIXES):
+        return False
+    if value_looks_sensitive(raw_value):
+        return False
+    if name in SAFE_EXACT:
+        return True
+    return any(name.startswith(prefix) for prefix in SAFE_PREFIXES)
+
+
+def captured_block(captured: dict[str, str]) -> list[str]:
+    lines = [
+        CAPTURE_BEGIN,
+        "// Generated from the local Firefox default profile by scripts/setup/firefox-capture-prefs.py.",
+        "// The helper excludes profile/session/device-specific prefs before writing this block.",
+    ]
+    for name in sorted(captured):
+        lines.append(f'user_pref("{name}", {captured[name]});')
+    lines.append(CAPTURE_END)
+    return lines
+
+
+def merge_user_js(user_js: Path, captured: dict[str, str]) -> int:
+    existing_lines = user_js.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
+    in_captured_block = False
+    outside_lines: list[str] = []
+    outside_pref_names: set[str] = set()
+    replaced_count = 0
+
+    for line in existing_lines:
+        stripped = line.strip()
+        if stripped == CAPTURE_BEGIN:
+            in_captured_block = True
+            continue
+        if stripped == CAPTURE_END:
+            in_captured_block = False
+            continue
+        if in_captured_block:
+            continue
+
+        match = USER_PREF_RE.match(line)
+        if match:
+            name = match.group("name")
+            outside_pref_names.add(name)
+            if name in captured and match.group("value") != captured[name]:
+                line = f'{match.group("prefix")}{captured[name]}{match.group("suffix")}'
+                replaced_count += 1
+        outside_lines.append(line)
+
+    remaining = {name: value for name, value in captured.items() if name not in outside_pref_names}
+    while outside_lines and outside_lines[-1] == "":
+        outside_lines.pop()
+
+    new_lines = outside_lines + ["", *captured_block(remaining), ""]
+    new_text = "\n".join(new_lines)
+    old_text = user_js.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+    if old_text != new_text:
+        user_js.write_text(new_text, encoding="utf-8")
+
+    return replaced_count
+
+
+def capture_prefs(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
+    firefox_root = Path(args.firefox_root).expanduser()
+    user_js = Path(args.user_js).expanduser()
+    profile = Path(args.profile).expanduser() if args.profile else find_default_profile(firefox_root)
+    prefs_js = profile / "prefs.js"
+
+    if not prefs_js.is_file():
+        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Firefox prefs.js not found: {prefs_js}")
+    if not user_js.is_file():
+        raise FileNotFoundError(f"dotfiles user.js not found: {user_js}")
+
+    prefs = parse_user_pref_lines(prefs_js)
+    captured = {name: value for name, value in prefs.items() if is_safe_pref(name, value)}
+
+    if args.dry_run:
+        print(f"profile={profile}")
+        print(f"prefs_seen={len(prefs)}")
+        print(f"prefs_captured={len(captured)}")
+        for name in sorted(captured):
+            print(name)
+        return 0
+
+    replaced = merge_user_js(user_js, captured)
+    print(f"Captured {len(captured)} safe Firefox prefs from {profile}")
+    if replaced:
+        print(f"Updated {replaced} existing user.js pref values")
+    print(f"Updated {user_js}")
+    return 0
+
+
+def self_test() -> int:
+    tmp = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp())
+    try:
+        firefox_root = tmp / "Firefox"
+        profile = firefox_root / "Profiles/test.default"
+        profile.mkdir(parents=True)
+        (firefox_root / "profiles.ini").write_text(
+            "\n".join(
+                [
+                    "[Install123]",
+                    "Default=Profiles/test.default",
+                    "[Profile0]",
+                    "Name=default",
+                    "IsRelative=1",
+                    "Path=Profiles/test.default",
+                    "Default=1",
+                    "",
+                ]
+            ),
+            encoding="utf-8",
+        )
+        (profile / "prefs.js").write_text(
+            "\n".join(
+                [
+                    'user_pref("browser.contentblocking.category", "strict");',
+                    'user_pref("browser.download.lastDir", "/Users/example/Downloads");',
+                    'user_pref("browser.shell.checkDefaultBrowser", true);',
+                    'user_pref("network.dns.disablePrefetch", true);',
+                    'user_pref("services.sync.username", "person@example.com");',
+                    "",
+                ]
+            ),
+            encoding="utf-8",
+        )
+        user_js = tmp / "user.js"
+        user_js.write_text(
+            "\n".join(
+                [
+                    "// Dotfiles-managed Firefox preferences.",
+                    'user_pref("browser.shell.checkDefaultBrowser", false);',
+                    "",
+                ]
+            ),
+            encoding="utf-8",
+        )
+
+        args = argparse.Namespace(
+            firefox_root=str(firefox_root),
+            profile="",
+            user_js=str(user_js),
+            dry_run=False,
+        )
+        capture_prefs(args)
+        output = user_js.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+
+        assert 'user_pref("browser.contentblocking.category", "strict");' in output
+        assert 'user_pref("browser.shell.checkDefaultBrowser", true);' in output
+        assert 'user_pref("network.dns.disablePrefetch", true);' in output
+        assert "browser.download.lastDir" not in output
+        assert "services.sync.username" not in output
+        assert CAPTURE_BEGIN in output and CAPTURE_END in output
+        return 0
+    finally:
+        shutil.rmtree(tmp)
+
+
+def parse_args(argv: list[str]) -> argparse.Namespace:
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
+    parser.add_argument(
+        "--firefox-root",
+        default=str(Path.home() / "Library/Application Support/Firefox"),
+        help="Firefox profile root containing profiles.ini",
+    )
+    parser.add_argument("--profile", default="", help="Firefox profile directory to read")
+    parser.add_argument(
+        "--user-js",
+        default=str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent / "firefox/user.js"),
+        help="dotfiles-managed user.js to update",
+    )
+    parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true", help="list captured pref names without writing")
+    parser.add_argument("--self-test", action="store_true", help="run synthetic parser/merge tests")
+    return parser.parse_args(argv)
+
+
+def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
+    args = parse_args(argv)
+    if args.self_test:
+        return self_test()
+    return capture_prefs(args)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main(sys.argv[1:]))
*** End Patch

+    profile_path = Path(profile_ref)
+    if profile_path.is_absolute():
+        return profile_path
+    return firefox_root / profile_path

+
+def find_default_profile(firefox_root: Path) -> Path:
+    profiles_ini = firefox_root / "profiles.ini"
+    if not profiles_ini.is_file():
+        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Firefox profiles.ini not found: {profiles_ini}")
+
+    section = ""
+    path = ""
+    is_relative = "1"
+    is_default = "0"
+    install_default = ""
+    profile_default: tuple[str, str] | None = None
+    first_profile: tuple[str, str] | None = None
+
+    def commit_profile_section() -> None:
+        nonlocal first_profile, profile_default
+        if not section.startswith("Profile") or not path:
+            return
+        if first_profile is None:
+            first_profile = (path, is_relative)
+        if is_default == "1":
+            profile_default = (path, is_relative)
+
+    with profiles_ini.open("r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as handle:
+        for raw_line in handle:
+            line = raw_line.rstrip("\n").rstrip("\r")
+            if not line or line.startswith(("#", ";")):
+                continue
+            if line.startswith("[") and line.endswith("]"):
+                commit_profile_section()
+                section = line[1:-1]
+                path = ""
+                is_relative = "1"
+                is_default = "0"
+                continue
+
+            key, sep, value = line.partition("=")
+            if not sep:
+                continue
+            if section.startswith("Install") and key == "Default":
+                install_default = value
+            elif section.startswith("Profile"):
+                if key == "Path":
+                    path = value
+                elif key == "IsRelative":
+                    is_relative = value
+                elif key == "Default" and value == "1":
+                    is_default = "1"
+
+    commit_profile_section()
+
+    if install_default:
+        return resolve_profile_path(firefox_root, install_default)
+    if profile_default:
+        path, relative = profile_default
+        return resolve_profile_path(firefox_root, path) if relative == "1" else Path(path)
+    if first_profile:
+        path, relative = first_profile
+        return resolve_profile_path(firefox_root, path) if relative == "1" else Path(path)
+
+    raise FileNotFoundError(f"No Firefox profiles found in {profiles_ini}")
+

+def value_looks_sensitive(raw_value: str) -> bool:
+    lowered = raw_value.lower()
+    home = str(Path.home()).lower()
+    sensitive_fragments = (
+        home,
+        "/users/",
+        "file://",
+        "token",
+        "secret",
+        "password",
+        "credential",
+        "clientid",
+        "guid",
+        "uuid",

+    if name in DENY_EXACT:
+        return False
+    if any(name.startswith(prefix) for prefix in DENY_PREFIXES):
+        return False
+    if value_looks_sensitive(raw_value):
+        return False
+    if name in SAFE_EXACT:
+        return True
+    return any(name.startswith(prefix) for prefix in SAFE_PREFIXES)
